text: 1) Earth and Space – Multiple Choice Which of the following stars has a positive apparent magnitude? W) Polaris X) Sirius Y) Canopus Z) The Sun
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 1) Earth and Space – Multiple Choice Which of the following assumptions is typically made when performing potassium-argon dating? W) Potassium-40 only decays into argon-40 X) Some percentage of argon-40 produced by decay leaks out of the rock Y) Potassium-40 has a half-life of 1.3 million years Z) All argon-40 in a rock was produced by potassium-40 decay
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Z) ALL ARGON-40 IN A ROCK WAS PRODUCED BY POTASSIUM-40 DECAY
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 2) Math – Short Answer A set of test scores is normally distributed with mean 62 and standard deviation 8. What score corresponds to a Z-score of –1.25?

text: 3) Chemistry – Short Answer Grignard reagents are generally made in ether-based solvents due to the instability of the metal-organic species. Identify all of the following three solvents that are considered ethereal: 1) Dioxane; 2) Tetrahydrofuran; 3) Dichloromethane.
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: 4) Physics – Multiple Choice An electron is moving along the x-axis in the positive direction, within a uniform magnetic field that is oriented toward the positive z-axis. In which of the following directions does the Lorentz force act? W) Positive y-axis X) Negative y-axis Y) Positive z-axis Z) Negative z-axis
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 6) Energy – Short Answer Oak Ridge National Lab is contributing to the production of cancer radiation treatments by producing actinium-227, which undergoes two decay transitions to become the medically-useful isotope radium-223. What two types of decay does actinium-227 undergo to become radium-223?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: BETA AND ALPHA (ACCEPT: ALPHA AND BETA)
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 6) Energy – Short Answer Scientists at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab have been studying crystallized biomolecules with electron microscopy. In one type of experiment, the samples are immersed in liquid nitrogen to form vitreous ice before imaging. What type of electron microscopy is this?

text: 9) Energy – Short Answer Scientists at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab are developing thermoelectric materials that can convert thermal energy to electrical energy via what effect?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: SEEBECK EFFECT (ACCEPT: PELTIER-SEEBECK EFFECT, DO NOT ACCEPT: PELTIER EFFECT)

text: 10) Math – Short Answer f(x) is a twice-differentiable function such that f'(x) and f''(x) are both less than 0 for all real numbers. If f(3) = 22 and f(5) = 17, what is the largest possible integer value for f(9)?

text: 11) Physics – Short Answer A reading of zero on what two temperature scales refers to absolute zero?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: KELVIN AND RANKINE (IN ANY ORDER)
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 12) Chemistry – Short Answer Identify all of the following three statements that are true of bonding: 1) Xenon dichloride has a bent molecular geometry; 2) Sulfur dioxide and carbon disulfide have the same molecular geometry; 3) Krypton tetrafluoride has no net dipole moment.

text: 14) Physics – Short Answer If the temperature of an ideal gas is quadrupled, by what factor is the speed of sound in that gas multiplied?

text: 14) Physics – Short Answer The position of a particle in meters is described by the function x(t) = t^3 + 4t^2 + 3t, where t is expressed in seconds. What is the acceleration, in meters per second squared, at time t = 5?

text: 15) Energy – Multiple Choice Scientists at Oak Ridge National Lab have designed a fuel rod cladding alloy made of iron, chromium, and aluminum to replace current zirconium alloys. Which of the following must be a property of this cladding? W) Converts fast neutrons into thermal neutrons X) Converts thermal neutrons into fast neutrons Y) Has a high neutron cross-section Z) Has a low neutron cross-section
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Z) HAS A LOW NEUTRON CROSS-SECTION
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 15) Energy – Short Answer Researchers at Oak Ridge National Lab have been examining how glaucoma drugs interact with a human enzyme that converts carbon dioxide and water to bicarbonate. What is the common name of this enzyme?
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: CARBONIC ANHYDRASE (ACCEPT: HUMAN CARBONIC ANHYDRASE)
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 16) Chemistry – Multiple Choice Adding ethyl-magnesium bromide to which of the following would produce a tertiary alcohol? W) A ketone X) An aldehyde Y) An alkene Z) An ether
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 16) Chemistry – Multiple Choice Which of the following statements best describes how chlorine adds to an alkene? W) Both chlorine atoms add to the same carbon atom X) Chlorine atoms add with syn-stereoselectivity to adjacent carbon atoms Y) Chlorine atoms add with anti-stereoselectivity to adjacent carbon atoms Z) Only one chlorine atom adds to the carbon atoms
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Y) CHLORINE ATOMS ADD WITH ANTI-STEREOSELECTIVITY TO ADJACENT CARBON ATOMS
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 17) Math – Short Answer If a pyramid has 8 vertices, how many edges does it have?

text: 18) Earth and Space – Multiple Choice A galaxy is receding from an observer such that the hydrogen alpha line appears to be at 657 nanometers instead of 656.3 nanometers. Which of the following best describes the changes in the beta and gamma lines for this galaxy? W) They will both be shifted longer by 0.7 nanometers X) They will both be shifted by slightly less than 0.7 nanometers Y) They will both be shifted by slightly more than 0.7 nanometers Z) They will both be shifted shorter by 0.7 nanometers
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) THEY WILL BOTH BE SHIFTED BY SLIGHTLY LESS THAN 0.7 NANOMETERS
label: BONUS_ANSWER
